metadata.name: SABIC CyberTrust Guidelines
metadata.description: The SABIC CyberTrust Guidelines establish cybersecurity requirements for SABIC and its contractors, suppliers, and partners to ensure the protection of SABIC's information assets and operational technology systems.
metadata.classification_reasoning: The SABIC CyberTrust Guidelines is a compliance framework established by SABIC for its supply chain and business partners. It mandates specific security controls that must be implemented to maintain business relationships with SABIC.
metadata.framework_purpose: To establish minimum cybersecurity requirements for SABIC's contractors, suppliers, and partners to protect SABIC's information assets, intellectual property, and operational technology from cyber threats.
metadata.framework_scope: All contractors, suppliers, third-party service providers, and business partners that access SABIC systems, handle SABIC data, or connect to SABIC networks.
framework_objectives: Protect SABIC information assets and intellectual property
Secure access to SABIC systems and networks
Ensure secure handling of SABIC data
Enable effective incident response and communication
metadata.target_audience: CISOs, IT Security Teams, Compliance Officers, and Vendor Managers of SABIC contractors, suppliers, and partners
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Non-compliance may result in termination of business relationship, contract penalties, and exclusion from future SABIC projects.
metadata.adoption_approach: Self-assessment with periodic audits. Contractors must demonstrate compliance through evidence submission and may be subject to SABIC security audits.

controls.title: Information Security Policy
controls.description: Establish and maintain an information security policy approved by management.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall establish and maintain an information security policy approved by senior management. The policy shall define the organization's approach to managing information security and shall be communicated to all employees and relevant external parties.

controls.title: Security Roles and Responsibilities
controls.description: Define and assign information security roles and responsibilities.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall define and assign information security roles and responsibilities. A designated individual shall be responsible for information security management, with clear accountability and authority to implement security controls.

controls.title: Security Awareness Training
controls.description: Provide security awareness training to all personnel.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall provide security awareness training to all personnel with access to SABIC information or systems. Training shall be provided upon hire and refreshed annually, covering topics relevant to the individual's role.

controls.title: Risk Assessment
controls.description: Conduct regular security risk assessments.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall conduct security risk assessments at least annually and when significant changes occur. Risk assessments shall identify threats, vulnerabilities, and potential impacts to SABIC-related information and systems.

controls.title: Asset Inventory
controls.description: Maintain inventory of assets that process or store SABIC information.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall maintain an accurate inventory of all assets that process, store, or transmit SABIC information. The inventory shall include hardware, software, and data assets with assigned owners.

controls.title: Information Classification
controls.description: Classify and label SABIC information according to sensitivity.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall classify and label SABIC information according to SABIC's classification scheme. Handling procedures shall be defined and implemented for each classification level.

controls.title: Media Handling
controls.description: Implement secure handling procedures for removable media.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement secure handling procedures for removable media containing SABIC information. Media shall be encrypted, tracked, and securely disposed of when no longer needed.

controls.title: Access Control Policy
controls.description: Establish and enforce access control policies.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall establish and enforce access control policies based on the principle of least privilege. Access shall be granted based on business need and shall be reviewed regularly.

controls.title: User Authentication
controls.description: Implement strong authentication for all users.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement strong authentication mechanisms for all users accessing systems with SABIC information. Multi-factor authentication shall be used for remote access and privileged accounts.

controls.title: Privileged Access Management
controls.description: Control and monitor privileged access accounts.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement privileged access management controls. Privileged accounts shall be inventoried, access tightly controlled, and activities monitored and logged.

controls.title: Access Revocation
controls.description: Promptly revoke access when no longer required.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall promptly revoke access to SABIC systems and information when personnel leave the organization or change roles. Access revocation shall occur within 24 hours of termination.

controls.title: Encryption Standards
controls.description: Use approved encryption standards for protecting SABIC data.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall use industry-approved encryption standards for protecting SABIC data at rest and in transit. Encryption algorithms shall meet current security requirements (e.g., AES-256, TLS 1.2+).

controls.title: Key Management
controls.description: Implement secure key management practices.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement secure key management practices covering key generation, distribution, storage, rotation, and destruction. Keys shall be protected from unauthorized access.

controls.title: Physical Access Controls
controls.description: Implement physical access controls for facilities with SABIC systems.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement physical access controls for facilities housing systems that process or store SABIC information. Access shall be restricted to authorized personnel with logging of entry and exit.

controls.title: Equipment Protection
controls.description: Protect equipment from physical and environmental threats.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall protect equipment from physical and environmental threats including theft, fire, flood, and power disruption. Environmental controls shall be appropriate for the equipment type.

controls.title: Secure Disposal
controls.description: Securely dispose of equipment containing SABIC data.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall securely dispose of equipment containing SABIC data. Storage media shall be securely wiped or physically destroyed. Certificates of destruction shall be maintained.

controls.title: Change Management
controls.description: Implement change management for systems handling SABIC data.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement change management processes for systems handling SABIC data. Changes shall be documented, tested, and approved before implementation.

controls.title: Malware Protection
controls.description: Implement malware protection on all systems.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement malware protection on all systems that process or store SABIC information. Anti-malware solutions shall be kept current and configured for real-time protection.

controls.title: Backup and Recovery
controls.description: Implement backup and recovery procedures for SABIC data.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement backup and recovery procedures for SABIC data. Backups shall be performed regularly, stored securely, and tested for recoverability.

controls.title: Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Implement vulnerability management for systems with SABIC data.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement vulnerability management for systems processing or storing SABIC data. Vulnerabilities shall be identified through regular scanning and remediated within defined timeframes.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring
controls.description: Implement logging and monitoring for security events.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement logging and monitoring for security events on systems with SABIC data. Logs shall be protected, retained for the required period, and reviewed regularly for anomalies.

controls.title: Network Segmentation
controls.description: Implement network segmentation for systems with SABIC data.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement network segmentation to isolate systems processing SABIC data from untrusted networks. Firewalls and access controls shall be implemented at segment boundaries.

controls.title: Secure Data Transfer
controls.description: Encrypt data transfers containing SABIC information.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall encrypt all data transfers containing SABIC information over untrusted networks. Secure protocols (e.g., SFTP, HTTPS) shall be used for data exchange.

controls.title: Remote Access Security
controls.description: Implement secure remote access controls.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement secure remote access controls for accessing systems with SABIC data. VPN or other encrypted access methods shall be used, with multi-factor authentication required.

controls.title: Email Security
controls.description: Implement email security controls for SABIC communications.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall implement email security controls including encryption for sensitive information, spam filtering, and phishing protection. Users shall be trained on email security risks.

controls.title: Incident Response Plan
controls.description: Establish incident response plan for security incidents.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall establish an incident response plan for security incidents affecting SABIC data or systems. The plan shall define roles, procedures, and communication protocols.

controls.title: Incident Notification to SABIC
controls.description: Notify SABIC of security incidents within defined timeframes.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall notify SABIC of security incidents affecting SABIC data or systems within 24 hours of detection. Notifications shall include incident details, impact assessment, and containment actions.

controls.title: Incident Documentation
controls.description: Document and retain records of security incidents.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall document and retain records of security incidents affecting SABIC data or systems. Documentation shall include incident timeline, actions taken, and lessons learned.

controls.title: Business Continuity Planning
controls.description: Include SABIC-related services in business continuity planning.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall include SABIC-related services in their business continuity planning. Recovery procedures shall be documented and tested to ensure continuity of services to SABIC.

controls.title: Disaster Recovery
controls.description: Establish disaster recovery capabilities for critical SABIC systems.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall establish disaster recovery capabilities for critical systems supporting SABIC services. Recovery time and point objectives shall be defined and tested.

controls.title: Compliance with SABIC Requirements
controls.description: Ensure ongoing compliance with SABIC CyberTrust requirements.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall ensure ongoing compliance with SABIC CyberTrust requirements. Compliance shall be assessed regularly through self-assessments and addressed through remediation plans.

controls.title: Legal and Regulatory Compliance
controls.description: Comply with applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall comply with applicable legal and regulatory requirements related to information security and data protection. Compliance requirements shall be identified and monitored.

controls.title: Security Audits
controls.description: Support SABIC security audits when requested.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall support SABIC security audits when requested. Access shall be provided to facilities, systems, and documentation as required for audit purposes.

controls.title: Subcontractor Compliance
controls.description: Ensure subcontractors comply with SABIC CyberTrust requirements.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall ensure that subcontractors handling SABIC data or accessing SABIC systems comply with SABIC CyberTrust requirements. Subcontractor compliance shall be contractually required and verified.

controls.title: Continuous Improvement
controls.description: Continuously improve information security practices.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall continuously improve their information security practices. Improvement opportunities shall be identified through incident analysis, audits, and security assessments, and implemented through a formal improvement process.
